title: Authority Reconstruction Framework
description: Defines the authoritative lineage ordering for state reconstruction during recovery. Specifies trust hierarchy, reconstruction procedure, conflict resolution, and replay guarantees.
purpose: Enable deterministic reconstruction of runtime state from authoritative sources during recovery, eliminating ambiguity about which source of truth to trust.

invariants.description: Trust hierarchy must form a strict total order with no ambiguous rankings.
Append-only journal must never be mutated, truncated, or reordered.
All checkpoints must validate against checkpoint schema before use as reconstruction anchors.

trust_hierarchy.description: Ordered list of state sources from highest to lowest authority. Lower-authority sources are derived from higher-authority sources.

levels.description: Immutable append-only event streams. Every governance event is recorded with monotonic timestamps.
Checkpoint files created at known-good states. Form the recovery backbone.
Execution transition logs recording every state mutation with before/after snapshots.
The single mutable runtime state file. All other state files are derived from this.
Active execution context and runtime state files. Derived from canonical state.
Derived read models, indexes, and cached artifacts. Lowest authority.

reconstruction_procedure.description: Deterministic procedure for reconstructing runtime state from authoritative sources.

phases.name: journal_replay
phases.description: Replay all events from append-only journal in timestamp order. Build event-derived state.
phases.determinism: strict: events replayed in exact order, no skipping, no reordering

phases.name: checkpoint_anchor
phases.description: Identify most recent valid checkpoint. Anchor reconstruction at this known-good state.
phases.determinism: strict: select latest checkpoint with valid schema and unbroken lineage

phases.name: transition_replay
phases.description: Replay transitions from checkpoint timestamp to present. Apply each transition's before/after delta.
phases.determinism: strict: transitions applied in sequence, gaps flagged for investigation

phases.name: canonical_reconcile
phases.description: Reconcile reconstructed state with canonical-state.json. Use canonical state only for values not derivable from higher authorities.
phases.determinism: deterministic: canonical values overridden by higher-authority sources

phases.name: runtime_derive
phases.description: Derive runtime memory state from canonical state. Rebuild active-execution, runtime-state, current-ticket.
phases.determinism: deterministic: pure functions from canonical state to runtime surfaces

phases.name: projection_rebuild
phases.description: Recompute all projections from canonical and runtime state. Invalidate stale caches.
phases.determinism: deterministic: idempotent rebuild from higher-authority sources

conflict_resolution.description: Rules for resolving conflicts between state sources at different authority levels.

rules.description: When two sources disagree, the higher-ranked source in the trust hierarchy is authoritative.
rules.example: If journal shows T27.1 completed but canonical-state shows T27.1 executing, journal wins.

rules.description: The append-only journal is the ultimate source of truth. Any state not consistent with the journal is considered drift.
rules.exception: Journal corruption requires manual intervention with explicit --destructive flag.

rules.description: Checkpoints are valid anchors only if they validate against checkpoint.schema.json and have unbroken lineage.
rules.invalid_action: Invalid checkpoints are skipped; reconstruction continues from next valid checkpoint.

rules.description: Gaps in transition logs or journal streams are flagged as HIGH severity drift. Reconstruction may proceed but must emit gap events.
rules.action: Emit governance event: recovery.gap_detected. Log missing sequence numbers.

rules.description: canonical-state.json is a derived projection of the journal + checkpoints + transitions. It may be rebuilt from higher authorities.
rules.action: If canonical state conflicts with journal, rebuild canonical state from journal replay.

replay_guarantees.description: Guarantees that reconstruction replay must satisfy.

guarantees.description: Same inputs (journal + checkpoints + transitions) must always produce identical reconstructed state.
Checkpoint lineage must be monotonic. No checkpoint may reference a future state.
Events replay in exact timestamp order. Concurrent events at identical timestamps use sequence numbers.
Projection rebuild is idempotent. Rebuilding from same canonical state produces identical projections.
